title: Crystalline DNA
wikitext:
[]: "Crystalline DNA"?             Quote Post by felineki » Mon Aug 10, 2009 11:49 pm Was looking at the compiled and translated Chronicle glossary on the wiki and noticed the "Crystalline DNA Argument" ("Kesshou Idenshi Ron") entry. Didn't sound particularly familiar to me, so I searched the forum for discussions relating to it, but couldn't find any (if it was previously discussed, please point me in the proper direction). So, using my budding crappy Japanese skills I did some Googling on the Japanese term and found out that it apparently is featured in the rapid-fire sequence of documents/flashback that occurs as part of Shinji's mental trip in episode 16. The term's real-world relevance seems to be in reference to a theory created by Graham Cairns-Smith . With regard to it's relevance to NGE, various sites seem to take the view that is has something to do with the unique composition of the Angels, so it might be related to the subject we usually refer to as "Particle Wave Matter". Any thoughts? For kicks, here's another part of one of the Japanese sites referred to earlier that attempts to document and analyze everything in that quick flashback montage. I had actually been hoping to come across something like this for the "Reiquarium" images, but have yet to find anything. Although the Japanese fans seem to be just as mystified as we are about their removal from the Renewal version...

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Sat Aug 15, 2009 9:42 am Um...for Reiquarium shots and analysis- [url]http://www.evacommentary.org/episode-23/23_reiquarium.html[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sat Aug 15, 2009 9:52 am Correct me if i'm wrong, but the Wikipedia article talks about a possible method on how DNA might have formed, not an alternative form of DNA. Guess it's just another red herring.

[]:             Quote Post by felineki » Sat Aug 15, 2009 12:02 pm Teague wrote: Um...for Reiquarium shots and analysis- [url]http://www.evacommentary.org/episode-23/23_reiquarium.html[/url]

[]: "Crystalline DNA"?             Quote Post by felineki » Mon Aug 10, 2009 11:49 pm Was looking at the compiled and translated Chronicle glossary on the wiki and noticed the "Crystalline DNA Argument" ("Kesshou Idenshi Ron") entry. Didn't sound particularly familiar to me, so I searched the forum for discussions relating to it, but couldn't find any (if it was previously discussed, please point me in the proper direction). So, using my budding crappy Japanese skills I did some Googling on the Japanese term and found out that it apparently is featured in the rapid-fire sequence of documents/flashback that occurs as part of Shinji's mental trip in episode 16. The term's real-world relevance seems to be in reference to a theory created by Graham Cairns-Smith . With regard to it's relevance to NGE, various sites seem to take the view that is has something to do with the unique composition of the Angels, so it might be related to the subject we usually refer to as "Particle Wave Matter". Any thoughts? For kicks, here's another part of one of the Japanese sites referred to earlier that attempts to document and analyze everything in that quick flashback montage. I had actually been hoping to come across something like this for the "Reiquarium" images, but have yet to find anything. Although the Japanese fans seem to be just as mystified as we are about their removal from the Renewal version...
